creator: 2916869006@qq.com
text:
Mongodb (4.4.18) data loss after freebsd reboot.

I use VMware to run Free BSD. When I configure the image and export it to someone else for use, some people may find that reboot the image will cause the mongodb database to be droped.
      
# mongod.conf

# for documentation of all options, see:
#   http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/configuration-options/

# where to write logging data.
systemLog:
  destination: file
  logAppend: true
  path: /var/db/mongodb/mongod.log 

# Where and how to store data.
storage:
  dbPath: /var/db/mongodb 
  journal:
    enabled: true
#  engine:
#  wiredTiger:

# how the process runs
processManagement:
  fork: true  # fork and run in background
  pidFilePath: /var/db/mongodb/mongod.lock  # location of pidfile
  timeZoneInfo: /usr/share/zoneinfo

# network interfaces
net:
  port: 27017
  bindIp: 127.0.0.1  # Enter 0.0.0.0,:: to bind to all IPv4 and IPv6 addresses or, alternatively, use the net.bindIpAll setting.


#security:

#operationProfiling:

replication:
  replSetName: rs0
